reason: ADR.md delivers 14 architecture decisions (ADR-001 ~ ADR-014) covering all major design surfaces called out by SAD.md §2 (module dependency graph), §3 (interfaces/data flows) and §4 (NFR handling). Each ADR carries the four canonical sections required by harness/templates/ADR.md (Status/Context/Decision/Consequences) with a consistent Alternatives Considered block (≥2 options per ADR), giving the review traceability needed for downstream checkers (e.g. check_nfr_adr_coverage). The FR/NFR traceability tables at the end are correctly populated: NFR-01 is now anchored by ADR-014 (cross-cutting) plus ADR-003, addressing the gap surfaced in the prior P2 round (Sync phase flagged NFR-01 as missing an owner ADR). High-Risk Module marking (executor/store) is explicitly carried in ADR-002 and match
